name: Breadboard Power Supply
tagline: Dual-output 3.3V and 5V regulated power supply that plugs directly into a breadboard
description: The MB102 breadboard power supply module plugs directly onto the power rails of a standard breadboard and provides selectable 3.3V or 5V regulated output on each rail independently. It accepts input via a DC barrel jack (6.5V–12V) or USB. Ideal for powering Arduino shields, sensor modules, and ICs directly from the breadboard without needing extra wiring.
wiringNote: Set the voltage jumpers BEFORE powering on. Left and right rails are independently configurable. Always connect GND of this module to Arduino GND when sharing power.
commonMistakes: Not checking jumper settings before powering on → wrong voltage damages 3.3V components
Forgetting to connect module GND to Arduino GND → floating ground causes erratic behavior
Using input voltage below 6.5V from barrel jack → regulator doesn't work correctly
